text: The book of the generation of Jesus Christ, the son of David, the son of Abraham:

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Abraham begot Isaac. And Isaac begot Jacob. And Jacob begot Judas and his brethren.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And Judas begot Phares and Zara of Thamar. And Phares begot Esron. And Esron begot Aram.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And Aram begot Aminadab. And Aminadab begot Naasson. And Naasson begot Salmon.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And Salmon begot Booz of Rahab. And Booz begot Obed of Ruth. And Obed begot Jesse.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And Jesse begot David the king. And David the king begot Solomon, of her that had been the wife of Urias.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And Solomon begot Roboam. And Roboam begot Abia. And Abia begot Asa.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And Asa begot Josaphat. And Josaphat begot Joram. And Joram begot Ozias.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And Ozias begot Joatham. And Joatham begot Achaz. And Achaz begot Ezechias.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And Ezechias begot Manasses. And Manasses begot Amon. And Amon begot Josias.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And Josias begot Jechonias and his brethren in the transmigration of Babylon.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And after the transmigration of Babylon, Jechonias begot Salathiel. And Salathiel begot Zorobabel.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And Zorobabel begot Abiud. And Abiud begot Eliacim. And Eliacim begot Azor.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And Azor begot Sadoc. And Sadoc begot Achim. And Achim begot Eliud.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And Eliud begot Eleazar. And Eleazar begot Mathan. And Mathan begot Jacob.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And Jacob begot Joseph the husband of Mary, of whom was born Jesus, who is called Christ.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: So all the generations from Abraham to David, are fourteen generations. And from David to the transmigration of Babylon, are fourteen generations: and from the transmigration of Babylon to Christ are fourteen generations.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Now the generation of Christ was in this wise. When as his mother Mary was espoused to Joseph, before they came together, she was found with child, of the Holy Ghost.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Whereupon Joseph her husband, being a just man, and not willing publicly to expose her, was minded to put her away privately.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: But while he thought on these things, behold the Angel of the Lord appeared to him in his sleep, saying: Joseph, son of David, fear not to take unto thee Mary thy wife, for that which is conceived in her, is of the Holy Ghost.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And she shall bring forth a son: and thou shalt call his name Jesus. For he shall save his people from their sins.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Now all this was done that it might be fulfilled which the Lord spoke by the prophet, saying:

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Behold a virgin shall be with child, and bring forth a son, and they shall call his name Emmanuel, which being interpreted is, God with us.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And Joseph rising up from sleep, did as the angel of the Lord had commanded him, and took unto him his wife.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And he knew her not till she brought forth her first born son: and he called his name Jesus.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: When Jesus therefore was born in Bethlehem of Juda, in the days of king Herod, behold, there came wise men from the East to Jerusalem,

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Saying: Where is he that is born king of the Jews? For we have seen his star in the East, and are come to adore him.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And king Herod hearing this, was troubled, and all Jerusalem with him.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And assembling together all the chief priests and the scribes of the people, he inquired of them where Christ should be born.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: But they said to him: In Bethlehem of Juda. For so it is written by the prophet:

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And thou Bethlehem the land of Juda art not the least among the princes of Juda: for out of thee shall come forth the captain that shall rule my people Israel.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Then Herod, privately calling the wise men learned diligently of them the time of the star which appeared to them;

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And sending them into Bethlehem, said: Go and diligently inquire after the child, and when you have found him, bring me word again, that I also may come and adore him.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Who having heard the king, went their way; and behold the star which they had seen in the East, went before them, until it came and stood over where the child was.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And seeing the star they rejoiced with exceeding great joy.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And entering into the house, they found the child with Mary his mother, and falling down they adored him: and opening their treasures, they offered him gifts; gold, frankincense, and myrrh.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And having received an answer in sleep that they should not return to Herod, they went back another way into their country.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And after they were departed, behold an angel of the Lord appeared in sleep to Joseph, saying: Arise, and take the child and his mother, and fly into Egypt: and be there until I shall tell thee. For it will come to pass that Herod will seek the child to destroy him.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Who arose, and took the child and his mother by night, and retired into Egypt: and he was there until the death of Herod:

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: That it might be fulfilled which the Lord spoke by the prophet, saying: Out of Egypt have I called my son.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Then Herod perceiving that he was deluded by the wise men, was exceeding angry: and sending killed all the menchildren that were in Bethlehem, and in all the borders thereof, from two years old and under, according to the time which he had diligently inquired of the wise men.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Then was fulfilled that which was spoken by Jeremias the prophet, saying:

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: A voice in Rama was heard, lamentation and great mourning; Rachel bewailing her children, and would not be comforted, because they are not.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: But when Herod was dead, behold an angel of the Lord appeared in sleep to Joseph in Egypt,

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Saying: Arise, and take the child and his mother, and go into the land of Israel. For they are dead that sought the life of the child.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Who arose, and took the child and his mother, and came into the land of Israel.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: But hearing that Archelaus reigned in Judea in the room of Herod his father, he was afraid to go thither: and being warned in sleep retired into the quarters of Galilee.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And coming he dwelt in a city called Nazareth: that it might be fulfilled which was said by the prophets: That he shall be called a Nazarene.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And in those days cometh John the Baptist preaching in the desert of Judea.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And saying: Do penance: for the kingdom of heaven is at hand.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: For this is he that was spoken of by Isaias the prophet, saying: A voice of one crying in the desert, Prepare ye the way of the Lord, make straight his paths.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And the same John had his garment of camel’s hair, and a leathern girdle about his loins: and his meat was locusts and wild honey.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Then went out to him Jerusalem and all Judea, and all the country about Jordan:

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And were baptized by him in the Jordan, confessing their sins.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And seeing many of the Pharisees and Sadducees coming to his baptism, he said to them: Ye brood of vipers, who hath shewed you to flee from the wrath to come?

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Bring forth therefore fruit worthy of penance.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And think not to say within yourselves, We have Abraham for our father. For I tell you that God is able of these stones to raise up children to Abraham.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: For now the axe is laid to the root of the trees. Every tree therefore that doth not yield good fruit, shall be cut down, and cast into the fire.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: I indeed baptize you in water unto penance, but he that shall come after me, is mightier than I, whose shoes I am not worthy to bear: he shall baptize you in the Holy Ghost and fire.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Whose fan is in his hand, and he will thoroughly cleanse his floor and gather his wheat into the barn; but the chaff he will burn with unquenchable fire.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Then cometh Jesus from Galilee to the Jordan, unto John, to be baptized by him.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: But John stayed him, saying: I ought to be baptized by thee, and comest thou to me?

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And Jesus answering, said to him: Suffer it to be so now. For so it becometh us to fulfil all justice. Then he suffered him.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And Jesus being baptized, forthwith came out of the water: and lo, the heavens were opened to him: and he saw the Spirit of God descending as a dove, and coming upon him.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And behold a voice from heaven saying: This is my beloved Son, in whom I am well pleased.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Then Jesus was led by the spirit into the desert, to be tempted by the devil.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And when he had fasted forty days and forty nights, afterwards he was hungry.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And the tempter coming said to him: If thou be the Son of God, command that these stones be made bread.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Who answered and said: It is written, Not in bread alone doth man live, but in every word that proceedeth from the mouth of God.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Then the devil took him up into the holy city, and set him upon the pinnacle of the temple,

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And said to him: If thou be the Son of God, cast thyself down, for it is written: That he hath given his angels charge over thee, and in their hands shall they bear thee up, lest perhaps thou dash thy foot against a stone.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Jesus said to him: It is written again: Thou shalt not tempt the Lord thy God.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Again the devil took him up into a very high mountain, and shewed him all the kingdoms of the world, and the glory of them,

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And said to him: All these will I give thee, if falling down thou wilt adore me.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Then Jesus saith to him: Begone, Satan: for it is written: The Lord thy God shalt thou adore, and him only shalt thou serve.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Then the devil left him; and behold angels came and ministered to him.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And when Jesus had heard that John was delivered up, he retired into Galilee:

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And leaving the city Nazareth, he came and dwelt in Capharnaum on the sea coast, in the borders of Zabulon and of Nephthalim;

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: That it might be fulfilled which was said by Isaias the prophet:

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Land of Zabulon and land of Nephthalim, the way of the sea beyond the Jordan, Galilee of the Gentiles:

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: The people that sat in darkness, hath seen great light: and to them that sat in the region of the shadow of death, light is sprung up.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: From that time Jesus began to preach, and to say: Do penance, for the kingdom of heaven is at hand.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And Jesus walking by the sea of Galilee, saw two brethren, Simon who is called Peter, and Andrew his brother, casting a net into the sea (for they were fishers).

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And he saith to them: Come ye after me, and I will make you to be fishers of men.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And they immediately leaving their nets, followed him.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And going on from thence, he saw other two brethren, James the son of Zebedee, and John his brother, in a ship with Zebedee their father, mending their nets: and he called them.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And they forthwith left their nets and father, and followed him.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And Jesus went about all Galilee, teaching in their synagogues, and preaching the gospel of the kingdom: and healing all manner of sickness and every infirmity, among the people.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And his fame went throughout all Syria, and they presented to him all sick people that were taken with divers diseases and torments, and such as were possessed by devils, and lunatics, and those that had the palsy, and he cured them:

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And much people followed him from Galilee, and from Decapolis, and from Jerusalem, and from Judea, and from beyond the Jordan.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And seeing the multitudes, he went up into a mountain, and when he was set down, his disciples came unto him.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And opening his mouth he taught them, saying:

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Blessed are the poor in spirit: for theirs is the kingdom of heaven.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Blessed are the meek: for they shall possess the land.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Blessed are they that mourn: for they shall be comforted.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Blessed are they that hunger and thirst after justice: for they shall have their fill.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Blessed are the merciful: for they shall obtain mercy.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Blessed are the clean of heart: for they shall see God.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Blessed are the peacemakers: for they shall be called the children of God.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Blessed are they that suffer persecution for justice’ sake: for theirs is the kingdom of heaven.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Blessed are ye when they shall revile you, and persecute you, and speak all that is evil against you, untruly, for my sake:

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Be glad and rejoice for your reward is very great in heaven. For so they persecuted the prophets that were before you.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: You are the salt of the earth. But if the salt lose its savour, wherewith shall it be salted? It is good for nothing anymore but to be cast out, and to be trodden on by men.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: You are the light of the world. A city seated on a mountain cannot be hid.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Neither do men light a candle and put it under a bushel, but upon a candlestick, that it may shine to all that are in the house.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: So let your light shine before men, that they may see your good works, and glorify your Father who is in heaven.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Do not think that I am come to destroy the law, or the prophets. I am not come to destroy, but to fulfil.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: For amen I say unto you, till heaven and earth pass, one jot, or one tittle shall not pass of the law, till all be fulfilled.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: He therefore that shall break one of these least commandments, and shall so teach men shall be called the least in the kingdom of heaven. But he that shall do and teach, he shall be called great in the kingdom of heaven.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: For I tell you, that unless your justice abound more than that of the scribes and Pharisees, you shall not enter into the kingdom of heaven.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: You have heard that it was said to them of old: Thou shalt not kill. And whosoever shall kill, shall be in danger of the judgment.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: But I say to you, that whosoever is angry with his brother, shall be in danger of the judgment. And whosoever shall say to his brother, Raca, shall be in danger of the council. And whosoever shall say, Thou fool, shall be in danger of hell fire.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: If therefore thou offer thy gift at the altar, and there thou remember that thy brother hath anything against thee;

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Leave there thy offering before the altar, and go first to be reconciled to thy brother, and then coming thou shalt offer thy gift.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Be at agreement with thy adversary betimes, whilst thou art in the way with him: lest perhaps the adversary deliver thee to the judge, and the judge deliver thee to the officer, and thou be cast into prison.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Amen I say to thee, thou shalt not go out from thence till thou repay the last farthing.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: You have heard that it was said to them of old: Thou shalt not commit adultery.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: But I say to you, that whosoever shall look on a woman to lust after her, hath already committed adultery with her in his heart.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And if thy right eye scandalize thee, pluck it out and cast it from thee. For it is expedient for thee that one of thy members should perish, rather than thy whole body be cast into hell.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And if thy right hand scandalize thee, cut it off, and cast it from thee: for it is expedient for thee that one of thy members should perish, rather than that thy whole body go into hell.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And it hath been said, Whosoever shall put away his wife, let him give her a bill of divorce.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: But I say to you, that whosoever shall put away his wife, excepting for the cause of fornication, maketh her to commit adultery: and he that shall marry her that is put away, committeth adultery.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Again you have heard that it was said to them of old, thou shalt not forswear thyself: but thou shalt perform thy oaths to the Lord.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: But I say to you not to swear at all, neither by heaven for it is the throne of God:

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Nor by the earth, for it is his footstool: nor by Jerusalem, for it is the city of the great king:

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Neither shalt thou swear by thy head, because thou canst not make one hair white or black.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: But let your speech be yea, yea: no, no: and that which is over and above these, is of evil.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: You have heard that it hath been said: An eye for an eye, and a tooth for a tooth.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: But I say to you not to resist evil: but if one strike thee on thy right cheek, turn to him also the other:

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And if a man will contend with thee in judgment, and take away thy coat, let go thy cloak also unto him.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And whosoever will force thee one mile, go with him other two.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Give to him that asketh of thee, and from him that would borrow of thee turn not away.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: You have heard that it hath been said, Thou shalt love thy neighbour, and hate thy enemy.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: But I say to you, Love your enemies: do good to them that hate you: and pray for them that persecute and calumniate you:

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: That you may be the children of your Father who is in heaven, who maketh his sun to rise upon the good, and bad, and raineth upon the just and the unjust.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: For if you love them that love you, what reward shall you have? do not even the publicans this?

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And if you salute your brethren only, what do you more? do not also the heathens this?

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Be you therefore perfect, as also your heavenly Father is perfect.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Take heed that you do not your justice before men, to be seen by them: otherwise you shall not have a reward of your Father who is in heaven.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Therefore when thou dost an alms-deed, sound not a trumpet before thee, as the hypocrites do in the synagogues and in the streets, that they may be honoured by men. Amen I say to you, they have received their reward.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: But when thou dost alms, let not thy left hand know what thy right hand doth.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: That thy alms may be in secret, and thy Father who seeth in secret will repay thee.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And when ye pray, you shall not be as the hypocrites, that love to stand and pray in the synagogues and corners of the streets, that they may be seen by men: Amen I say to you, they have received their reward.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: But thou when thou shalt pray, enter into thy chamber, and having shut the door, pray to thy Father in secret, and thy father who seeth in secret will repay thee.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And when you are praying, speak not much, as the heathens. For they think that in their much speaking they may be heard.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Be not you therefore like to them for your Father knoweth what is needful for you, before you ask him.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Thus therefore shall you pray: Our Father who art in heaven, hallowed be thy name.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Thy kingdom come. Thy will be done on earth as it is in heaven.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Give us this day our supersubstantial bread.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And forgive us our debts, as we also forgive our debtors.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And lead us not into temptation. But deliver us from evil. Amen.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: For if you will forgive men their offences, your heavenly Father will forgive you also your offences.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: But if you will not forgive men, neither will your Father forgive you your offences.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And when you fast, be not as the hypocrites, sad. For they disfigure their faces, that they may appear unto men to fast. Amen I say to you, they have received their reward.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: But thou, when thou fastest anoint thy head, and wash thy face;

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: That thou appear not to men to fast, but to thy Father who is in secret: and thy Father who seeth in secret, will repay thee.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Lay not up to yourselves treasures on earth: where the rust, and moth consume, and where thieves break through, and steal.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: But lay up to yourselves treasures in heaven: where neither the rust nor moth doth consume, and where thieves do not break through, nor steal.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: For where thy treasure is, there is thy heart also.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: The light of thy body is thy eye. If thy eye be single, thy whole body shall be lightsome.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: But if thy eye be evil thy whole body shall be darksome. If then the light that is in thee, be darkness: the darkness itself how great shall it be!

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: No man can serve two masters. For either he will hate the one, and love the other: or he will sustain the one, and despise the other. You cannot serve God and mammon.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Therefore I say to you, be not solicitous for your life, what you shall eat, nor for your body, what you shall put on. Is not the life more than the meat: and the body more than the raiment?

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Behold the birds of the air, for they neither sow, nor do they reap, nor gather into barns: and your heavenly Father feedeth them. Are not you of much more value than they?

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And which of you by taking thought, can add to his stature one cubit?

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And for raiment why are you solicitous? Consider the lilies of the field, how they grow: they labour not, neither do they spin.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: But I say to you, that not even Solomon in all his glory was arrayed as one of these.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And if the grass of the field, which is to day, and to morrow is cast into the oven, God doth so clothe: how much more you, O ye of little faith?

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Be not solicitous therefore, saying: What shall we eat: or what shall we drink, or wherewith shall we be clothed?

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: For after all these things do the heathens seek. For your Father knoweth that you have need of all these things.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Seek ye therefore first the kingdom of God, and his justice, and all these things shall be added unto you.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Be not therefore solicitous for to morrow; for the morrow will be solicitous for itself. Sufficient for the day is the evil thereof.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Judge not, that you may not be judged.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: For with what judgment you judge, you shall be judged: and with what measure you mete, it shall be measured to you again.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And why seest thou the mote that is in thy brother’s eye; and seest not the beam that is in thy own eye?

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Or how sayest thou to thy brother: Let me cast the mote out of thy eye; and behold a beam is in thy own eye?

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Thou hypocrite, cast out first the beam out of thy own eye, and then shalt thou see to cast out the mote out of thy brother’s eye.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Give not that which is holy to dogs; neither cast ye your pearls before swine, lest perhaps they trample them under their feet, and turning upon you, they tear you.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Ask, and it shall be given you: seek, and you shall find: knock, and it shall be opened to you.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: For every one that asketh, receiveth: and he that seeketh, findeth: and to him that knocketh, it shall be opened.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Or what man is there among you, of whom if his son shall ask bread, will he reach him a stone?

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Or if he shall ask him a fish, will he reach him a serpent?

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: If you then being evil, know how to give good gifts to your children: how much more will your Father who is in heaven, give good things to them that ask him?

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: All things therefore whatsoever you would that men should do to you, do you also to them. For this is the law and the prophets.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Enter ye in at the narrow gate: for wide is the gate, and broad is the way that leadeth to destruction, and many there are who go in thereat.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: How narrow is the gate, and strait is the way that leadeth to life: and few there are that find it!

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Beware of false prophets, who come to you in the clothing of sheep, but inwardly they are ravening wolves.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: By their fruits you shall know them. Do men gather grapes of thorns, or figs of thistles?

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Even so every good tree bringeth forth good fruit, and the evil tree bringeth forth evil fruit.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: A good tree cannot bring forth evil fruit, neither can an evil tree bring forth good fruit.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Every tree that bringeth not forth good fruit, shall be cut down, and shall be cast into the fire.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Wherefore by their fruits you shall know them.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Not every one that saith to me, Lord, Lord, shall enter into the kingdom of heaven: but he that doth the will of my Father who is in heaven, he shall enter into the kingdom of heaven.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Many will say to me in that day: Lord, Lord, have not we prophesied in thy name, and cast out devils in thy name, and done many miracles in thy name?

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And then will I profess unto them, I never knew you: depart from me, you that work iniquity.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Every one therefore that heareth these my words, and doth them, shall be likened to a wise man that built his house upon a rock,

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And the rain fell, and the floods came, and the winds blew, and they beat upon that house, and it fell not, for it was founded on a rock.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And every one that heareth these my words and doth them not, shall be like a foolish man that built his house upon the sand,

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And the rain fell, and the floods came, and the winds blew, and they beat upon that house, and it fell, and great was the fall thereof.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And it came to pass when Jesus had fully ended these words, the people were in admiration at his doctrine.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: For he was teaching them as one having power, and not as the scribes and Pharisees.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And when he was come down from the mountain, great multitudes followed him:

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And behold a leper came and adored him, saying: Lord, if thou wilt, thou canst make me clean.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And Jesus stretching forth his hand, touched him, saying: I will, be thou made clean. And forthwith his leprosy was cleansed.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And Jesus saith to him: See thou tell no man: but go, shew thyself to the priest, and offer the gift which Moses commanded, for a testimony unto them.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And when he had entered into Capharnaum, there came to him a centurion, beseeching him,

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And saying, Lord, my servant lieth at home sick of the palsy, and is grievously tormented.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And Jesus saith to him: I will come and heal him.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And the centurion, making answer, said: Lord, I am not worthy that thou shouldst enter under my roof; but only say the word, and my servant shall be healed.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: For I also am a man subject to authority, having under me soldiers; and I say to this, Go, and he goeth, and to another Come, and he cometh, and to my servant, Do this, and he doeth it.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And Jesus hearing this, marvelled; and said to them that followed him. Amen I say to you, I have not found so great faith in Israel.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And I say to you that many shall come from the east and the west, and shall sit down with Abraham, and Isaac and Jacob in the kingdom of heaven:

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: But the children of the kingdom shall be cast out into the exterior darkness: there shall be weeping and gnashing of teeth.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And Jesus said to the centurion: Go, and as thou hast believed, so be it done to thee. And the servant was healed at the same hour.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And when Jesus was come into Peter’s house, he saw his wife’s mother lying, and sick of a fever;

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And he touched her hand, and the fever left her, and she arose and ministered to them.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And when evening was come, they brought to him many that were possessed with devils: and he cast out the spirits with his word: and all that were sick he healed:

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: That it might be fulfilled, which was spoken by the prophet Isaias, saying: He took our infirmities, and bore our diseases.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And Jesus seeing great multitudes about him, gave orders to pass over the water.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And a certain scribe came and said to him: Master, I will follow thee whithersoever thou shalt go.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And Jesus saith to him: The foxes have holes, and the birds of the air nests; but the Son of man hath not where to lay his head.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And another of his disciples said to him: Lord, suffer me first to go and bury my father.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: But Jesus said to him: Follow me, and let the dead bury their dead.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And when he entered into the boat, his disciples followed him:

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And behold a great tempest arose in the sea, so that the boat was covered with waves, but he was asleep.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And they came to him, and awaked him, saying: Lord, save us, we perish.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And Jesus saith to them: Why are you fearful, O ye of little faith? Then rising up, he commanded the winds, and the sea, and there came a great calm.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: But the men wondered, saying: What manner of man is this, for the winds and the sea obey him?

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And when he was come on the other side of the water, into the country of the Gerasens, there met him two that were possessed with devils, coming out of the sepulchres, exceeding fierce, so that none could pass by that way.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And behold they cried out, saying: What have we to do with thee, Jesus Son of God? art thou come hither to torment us before the time?

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And there was, not far from them, a herd of many swine feeding.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And the devils besought him, saying: If thou cast us out hence, send us into the herd of swine.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And he said to them: Go. But they going out went into the swine, and behold the whole herd ran violently down a steep place into the sea: and they perished in the waters.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And they that kept them fled: and coming into the city, told every thing, and concerning them that had been possessed by the devils.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And behold the whole city went out to meet Jesus, and when they saw him, they besought him that he would depart from their coasts.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And entering into a boat, he passed over the water and came into his own city.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And behold they brought to him one sick of the palsy lying in a bed. And Jesus, seeing their faith, said to the man sick of the palsy: Be of good heart, son, thy sins are forgiven thee.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And behold some of the scribes said within themselves: He blasphemeth.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And Jesus seeing their thoughts, said: Why do you think evil in your hearts?

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Whether is easier, to say, Thy sins are forgiven thee: or to say, Arise, and walk?

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: But that you may know that the Son of man hath power on earth to forgive sins, (then said he to the man sick of the palsy,) Arise, take up thy bed, and go into thy house.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And he arose, and went into his house.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And the multitude seeing it, feared, and glorified God that gave such power to men.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And when Jesus passed on from thence, he saw a man sitting in the custom house, named Matthew; and he saith to him: Follow me. And he arose up and followed him.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And it came to pass as he was sitting at meat in the house, behold many publicans and sinners came, and sat down with Jesus and his disciples.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And the Pharisees seeing it, said to his disciples: Why doth your master eat with publicans and sinners?

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: But Jesus hearing it, said: They that are in health need not a physician, but they that are ill.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Go then and learn what this meaneth, I will have mercy and not sacrifice. For I am not come to call the just, but sinners.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Then came to him the disciples of John, saying: Why do we and the Pharisees, fast often, but thy disciples do not fast?

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And Jesus said to them: Can the children of the bridegroom mourn, as long as the bridegroom is with them? But the days will come, when the bridegroom shall be taken away from them, and then they shall fast.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And nobody putteth a piece of raw cloth unto an old garment. For it taketh away the fulness thereof from the garment, and there is made a greater rent.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Neither do they put new wine into old bottles. Otherwise the bottles break, and the wine runneth out, and the bottles perish. But new wine they put into new bottles: and both are preserved.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: As he was speaking these things unto them, behold a certain ruler came up, and adored him, saying: Lord, my daughter is even now dead; but come, lay thy hand upon her, and she shall live.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And Jesus rising up followed him, with his disciples.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And behold a woman who was troubled with an issue of blood twelve years, came behind him, and touched the hem of his garment.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: For she said within herself: If I shall touch only his garment, I shall be healed.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: But Jesus turning and seeing her, said: Be of good heart, daughter, thy faith hath made thee whole. And the woman was made whole from that hour.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And when Jesus was come into the house of the ruler, and saw the minstrels and the multitude making a rout,

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: He said: Give place, for the girl is not dead, but sleepeth. And they laughed him to scorn.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And when the multitude was put forth, he went in, and took her by the hand. And the maid arose.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And the fame hereof went abroad into all that country.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And as Jesus passed from thence, there followed him two blind men crying out and saying, Have mercy on us, O Son of David.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And when he was come to the house, the blind men came to him. And Jesus saith to them, Do you believe, that I can do this unto you? They say to him, Yea, Lord.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Then he touched their eyes, saying, According to your faith, be it done unto you.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And their eyes were opened, and Jesus strictly charged them, saying, See that no man know this.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: But they going out, spread his fame abroad in all that country.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And when they were gone out, behold they brought him a dumb man, possessed with a devil.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And after the devil was cast out, the dumb man spoke, and the multitudes wondered, saying, Never was the like seen in Israel.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: But the Pharisees said, By the prince of devils he casteth out devils.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And Jesus went about all the cities and towns, teaching in their synagogues, and preaching the gospel of the kingdom, and healing every disease, and every infirmity.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And seeing the multitudes, he had compassion on them: because they were distressed, and lying like sheep that have no shepherd.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Then he saith to his disciples, The harvest indeed is great, but the labourers are few.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Pray ye therefore the Lord of the harvest, that he send forth labourers into his harvest.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And having called his twelve disciples together, he gave them power over unclean spirits, to cast them out, and to heal all manner of diseases, and all manner of infirmities.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And the names of the twelve Apostles are these: The first, Simon who is called Peter, and Andrew his brother,

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: James the son of Zebedee, and John his brother, Philip and Bartholomew, Thomas and Matthew the publican, and James the son of Alpheus, and Thaddeus,

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Simon the Cananean, and Judas Iscariot, who also betrayed him.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: These twelve Jesus sent: commanding them, saying: Go ye not into the way of the Gentiles, and into the city of the Samaritans enter ye not.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: But go ye rather to the lost sheep of the house of Israel.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And going, preach, saying: The kingdom of heaven is at hand.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Heal the sick, raise the dead, cleanse the lepers, cast out devils: freely have you received, freely give.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Do not possess gold, nor silver, nor money in your purses:

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Nor scrip for your journey, nor two coats, nor shoes, nor a staff; for the workman is worthy of his meat.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And into whatsoever city or town you shall enter, inquire who in it is worthy, and there abide till you go thence.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And when you come into the house, salute it, saying: Peace be to this house.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And if that house be worthy, your peace shall come upon it; but if it be not worthy, your peace shall return to you.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And whosoever shall not receive you, nor hear your words: going forth out of that house or city shake off the dust from your feet.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Amen I say to you, it shall be more tolerable for the land of Sodom and Gomorrha in the day of judgment, than for that city.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Behold I send you as sheep in the midst of wolves. Be ye therefore wise as serpents and simple as doves.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: But beware of men. For they will deliver you up in councils, and they will scourge you in their synagogues.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And you shall be brought before governors, and before kings for my sake, for a testimony to them and to the Gentiles:

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: But when they shall deliver you up, take no thought how or what to speak: for it shall be given you in that hour what to speak:

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: For it is not you that speak, but the spirit of your Father that speaketh in you.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: The brother also shall deliver up the brother to death, and the father the son; and the children shall rise up against their parents, and shall put them to death.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And you shall be hated by all men for my name’s sake: but he that shall persevere unto the end, he shall be saved.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And when they shall persecute you in this city, flee into another. Amen I say to you, you shall not finish all the cities of Israel, till the Son of man come.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: The disciple is not above the master, nor the servant above his lord.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: It is enough for the disciple that he be as his master, and the servant as his lord. If they have called the good man of the house Beelzebub, how much more them of his household?

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Therefore fear them not. For nothing is covered that shall not be revealed: nor hid, that shall not be known.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: That which I tell you in the dark, speak ye in the light: and that which you hear in the ear, preach ye upon the housetops.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And fear ye not them that kill the body, and are not able to kill the soul: but rather fear him that can destroy both soul and body in hell.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Are not two sparrows sold for a farthing? and not one of them shall fall on the ground without your Father.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: But the very hairs of your head are all numbered.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Fear not therefore: better are you than many sparrows.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Every one therefore that shall confess me before men, I will also confess him before my Father who is in heaven.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: But he that shall deny me before men, I will also deny him before my Father who is in heaven.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Do not think that I came to send peace upon earth: I came not to send peace, but the sword.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: For I came to set a man at variance against his father, and the daughter against her mother, and the daughter in law against her mother in law.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And a man’s enemies shall be they of his own household.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: He that loveth father or mother more than me, is not worthy of me; and he that loveth son or daughter more than me, is not worthy of me.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And he that taketh not up his cross, and followeth me, is not worthy of me.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: He that findeth his life, shall lose it: and he that shall lose his life for me, shall find it.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: He that receiveth you, receiveth me: and he that receiveth me, receiveth him that sent me.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: He that receiveth a prophet in the name of a prophet, shall receive the reward of a prophet: and he that receiveth a just man in the name of a just man, shall receive the reward of a just man.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And whosoever shall give to drink to one of these little ones a cup of cold water only in the name of a disciple, amen I say to you he shall not lose his reward.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And it came to pass, when Jesus had made an end of commanding his twelve disciples, he passed from thence, to teach and to preach in their cities.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Now when John had heard in prison the works of Christ: sending two of his disciples he said to him:

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Art thou he that art to come, or look we for another?

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And Jesus making answer said to them: Go and relate to John what you have heard and seen.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: The blind see, the lame walk, the lepers are cleansed, the deaf hear, the dead rise again, the poor have the gospel preached to them.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And blessed is he that shall not be scandalized in me.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And when they went their way, Jesus began to say to the multitudes concerning John: What went you out into the desert to see? a reed shaken with the wind?

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: But what went you out to see? a man clothed in soft garments? Behold they that are clothed in soft garments, are in the houses of kings.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: But what went you out to see? A prophet? Yea I tell you, and more than a prophet.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: For this is he of whom it is written: Behold I send my angel before thy face, who shall prepare thy way before thee.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Amen I say to you, there hath not risen among them that are born of women a greater than John the Baptist: yet he that is the lesser in the kingdom of heaven is greater than he.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And from the days of John the Baptist until now, the kingdom of heaven suffereth violence, and the violent bear it away.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: For all the prophets and the law prophesied until John:

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And if you will receive it, he is Elias that is to come.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: He that hath ears to hear, let him hear.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: But whereunto shall I esteem this generation to be like? It is like to children sitting in the market place.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Who crying to their companions say: We have piped to you, and you have not danced: we have lamented, and you have not mourned.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: For John came neither eating nor drinking; and they say: He hath a devil.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: The Son of man came eating and drinking, and they say: Behold a man that is a glutton and a wine drinker, a friend of publicans and sinners. And wisdom is justified by her children.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Then began he to upbraid the cities wherein were done the most of his miracles, for that they had not done penance.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Woe to thee, Corozain, woe to thee, Bethsaida: for if in Tyre and Sidon had been wrought the miracles that have been wrought in you, they had long ago done penance in sackcloth and ashes.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: But I say unto you, it shall be more tolerable for Tyre and Sidon in the day of judgment, than for you.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And thou Capharnaum, shalt thou be exalted up to heaven? thou shalt go down even unto hell. For if in Sodom had been wrought the miracles that have been wrought in thee, perhaps it had remained unto this day.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: But I say unto you, that it shall be more tolerable for the land of Sodom in the day of judgment than for thee.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: At that time Jesus answered and said: I confess to thee, O Father, Lord of Heaven and earth, because thou hast hid these things from the wise and prudent, and hast revealed them to little ones.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Yea, Father: for so hath it seemed good in thy sight.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: All things are delivered to me by my Father. And no one knoweth the Son but the Father: neither doth any one know the Father, but the Son, and he to whom it shall please the Son to reveal him.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Come to me all you that labor and are burdened, and I will refresh you.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Take up my yoke upon you, and learn of me, because I am meek, and humble of heart: And you shall find rest to your souls.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: For my yoke is sweet and my burden light.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: At that time Jesus went through the corn on the sabbath: and his disciples being hungry, began to pluck the ears, and to eat.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And the Pharisees seeing them, said to him: Behold thy disciples do that which is not lawful to do on the sabbath days.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: But he said to them: Have you not read what David did when he was hungry, and they that were with him:

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: How he entered into the house of God, and did eat the loaves of proposition, which it was not lawful for him to eat, nor for them that were with him, but for the priests only?

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Or have ye not read in the law, that on the sabbath days the priests in the temple break the sabbath, and are without blame?

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: But I tell you that there is here a greater than the temple.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And if you knew what this meaneth: I will have mercy, and not sacrifice: you would never have condemned the innocent.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: For the Son of man is Lord even of the sabbath.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And when he had passed from thence, he came into their synagogues.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And behold there was a man who had a withered hand, and they asked him, saying: Is it lawful to heal on the sabbath days? that they might accuse him.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: But he said to them: What man shall there be among you, that hath one sheep: and if the same fall into a pit on the sabbath day, will he not take hold on it and lift it up?

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: How much better is a man than a sheep? Therefore it is lawful to do a good deed on the sabbath days.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Then he saith to the man: Stretch forth thy hand; and he stretched it forth, and it was restored to health even as the other.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And the Pharisees going out made a consultation against him, how they might destroy him.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: But Jesus knowing it, retired from thence: and many followed him, and he healed them all.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And he charged them that they should not make him known.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: That it might be fulfilled which was spoken by Isaias the prophet, saying:

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Behold my servant whom I have chosen, my beloved in whom my soul hath been well pleased. I will put my spirit upon him, and he shall shew judgment to the Gentiles.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: He shall not contend, nor cry out, neither shall any man hear his voice in the streets.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: The bruised reed he shall not break: and smoking flax he shall not extinguish: till he send forth judgment unto victory.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And in his name the Gentiles shall hope.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Then was offered to him one possessed with a devil, blind and dumb: and he healed him, so that he spoke and saw.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And all the multitudes were amazed, and said: Is not this the son of David?

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: But the Pharisees hearing it, said: This man casteth not out devils but by Beelzebub the prince of the devils.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And Jesus knowing their thoughts, said to them: Every kingdom divided against itself shall be made desolate: and every city or house divided against itself shall not stand.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And if Satan cast out Satan, he is divided against himself: how then shall his kingdom stand?

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And if I by Beelzebub cast out devils, by whom do your children cast them out? Therefore they shall be your judges.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: But if I by the Spirit of God cast out devils, then is the kingdom of God come upon you.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Or how can any one enter into the house of the strong, and rifle his goods, unless he first bind the strong? and then he will rifle his house.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: He that is not with me, is against me: and he that gathereth not with me, scattereth.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Therefore I say to you: Every sin and blasphemy shall be forgiven men, but the blasphemy of the Spirit shall not be forgiven.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And whosoever shall speak a word against the Son of man, it shall be forgiven him: but he that shall speak against the Holy Ghost, it shall not be forgiven him neither in this world, nor in the world to come.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Either make the tree good and its fruit good: or make the tree evil, and its fruit evil. For by the fruit the tree is known.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: O generation of vipers, how can you speak good things, whereas you are evil? for out of the abundance of the heart the mouth speaketh.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: A good man out of a good treasure bringeth forth good things: and an evil man out of an evil treasure bringeth forth evil things.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner
