text: But I say unto you, that every idle word that men shall speak, they shall render an account for it in the day of judgment.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: For by thy words thou shalt be justified, and by thy words thou shalt be condemned.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Then some of the scribes and Pharisees answered him, saying: Master, we would see a sign from thee.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Who answering said to them: An evil and adulterous generation seeketh a sign: and a sign shall not be given it, but the sign of Jonas the prophet.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: For as Jonas was in the whale’s belly three days and three nights: so shall the Son of man be in the heart of the earth three days and three nights.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: The men of Ninive shall rise in judgment with this generation, and shall condemn it: because they did penance at the preaching of Jonas. And behold a greater than Jonas here.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: The queen of the south shall rise in judgment with this generation, and shall condemn it: because she came from the ends of the earth to hear the wisdom of Solomon, and behold a greater than Solomon here.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And when an unclean spirit is gone out of a man he walketh through dry places seeking rest, and findeth none.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Then he saith: I will return into my house from whence I came out. And coming he findeth it empty, swept, and garnished.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Then he goeth, and taketh with him seven other spirits more wicked than himself, and they enter in and dwell there: and the last state of that man is made worse than the first. So shall it be also to this wicked generation.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: As he was yet speaking to the multitudes, behold his mother and his brethren stood without, seeking to speak to him.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And one said unto him: Behold thy mother and thy brethren stand without, seeking thee.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: But he answering him that told him, said: Who is my mother, and who are my brethren?

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And stretching forth his hand towards his disciples, he said: Behold my mother and my brethren.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: For whosoever shall do the will of my Father, that is in heaven, he is my brother, and sister, and mother.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: The same day Jesus going out of the house, sat by the sea side.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And great multitudes were gathered together unto him, so that he went up into a boat and sat: and all the multitude stood on the shore.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And he spoke to them many things in parables, saying: Behold the sower went forth to sow.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And whilst he soweth some fell by the way side, and the birds of the air came and ate them up.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And other some fell upon stony ground, where they had not much earth: and they sprung up immediately, because they had no deepness of earth.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And when the sun was up they were scorched: and because they had not root, they withered away.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And others fell among thorns: and the thorns grew up and choked them.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And others fell upon good ground: and they brought forth fruit, some an hundred fold, some sixty fold, and some thirty fold.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: He that hath ears to hear, let him hear.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And his disciples came and said to him: Why speakest thou to them in parables?

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Who answered and said to them: Because to you it is given to know the mysteries of the kingdom of heaven: but to them it is not given.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: For he that hath, to him shall be given, and he shall abound: but he that hath not, from him shall be taken away that also which he hath.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Therefore do I speak to them in parables: because seeing they see not, and hearing they hear not, neither do they understand.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And the prophecy of Isaias is fulfilled in them, who saith: By hearing you shall hear, and shall not understand: and seeing you shall see, and shall not perceive.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: For the heart of this people is grown gross, and with their ears they have been dull of hearing, and their eyes they have shut: lest at any time they should see with their eyes, and hear with their ears, and understand with their heart, and be converted, and I should heal them.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: But blessed are your eyes, because they see, and your ears, because they hear.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: For, amen, I say to you, many prophets and just men have desired to see the things that you see, and have not seen them: and to hear the things that you hear and have not heard them.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Hear you therefore the parable of the sower.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: When any one heareth the word of the kingdom, and understandeth it not, there cometh the wicked one, and catcheth away that which was sown in his heart: this is he that received the seed by the way side.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And he that received the seed upon stony ground, is he that heareth the word, and immediately receiveth it with joy.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Yet hath he not root in himself, but is only for a time: and when there ariseth tribulation and persecution because of the word, he is presently scandalized.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And he that received the seed among thorns, is he that heareth the word, and the care of this world and the deceitfulness of riches choketh up the word, and he becometh fruitless.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: But he that received the seed upon good ground, is he that heareth the word, and understandeth, and beareth fruit, and yieldeth the one an hundredfold, and another sixty, and another thirty.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Another parable he proposed to them, saying: The kingdom of heaven is likened to a man that sowed good seed in his field.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: But while men were asleep, his enemy came and oversowed cockle among the wheat and went his way.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And when the blade was sprung up, and had brought forth fruit, then appeared also the cockle.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And the servants of the good man of the house coming said to him. Sir, didst thou not sow good seed in thy field? Whence then hath it cockle?

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And he said to them: An enemy hath done this. And the servants said to him: Wilt thou that we go and gather it up?

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And he said: No, lest perhaps gathering up the cockle, you root up the wheat also together with it.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Suffer both to grow until the harvest, and in the time of the harvest I will say to the reapers: Gather up first the cockle, and bind it into bundles to burn, but the wheat gather ye into my barn.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Another parable he proposed unto them, saying: The kingdom of heaven is like to a grain of mustard seed, which a man took and sowed in his field.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Which is the least indeed of all seeds; but when it is grown up, it is greater than all herbs, and becometh a tree, so that the birds of the air come, and dwell in the branches thereof.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Another parable he spoke to them: The kingdom of heaven is like to leaven, which a woman took and hid in three measures of meal, until the whole was leavened.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: All these things Jesus spoke in parables to the multitudes: and without parables he did not speak to them.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: That it might be fulfilled which was spoken by the prophet, saying: I will open my mouth in parables, I will utter things hidden from the foundation of the world.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Then having sent away the multitudes, he came into the house, and his disciples came to him, saying: Expound to us the parable of the cockle of the field.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Who made answer and said to them: He that soweth the good seed is the Son of man.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And the field is the world. And the good seed are the children of the kingdom. And the cockle are the children of the wicked one.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And the enemy that sowed them, is the devil. But the harvest is the end of the world. And the reapers are the angels.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Even as cockle therefore is gathered up, and burnt with fire: so shall it be at the end of the world.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: The Son of man shall send his angels, and they shall gather out of his kingdom all scandals, and them that work iniquity.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And shall cast them into the furnace of fire: there shall be weeping and gnashing of teeth.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Then shall the just shine as the sun, in the kingdom of their Father. He that hath ears to hear, let him hear.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: The kingdom of heaven is like unto a treasure hidden in a field. Which a man having found, hid it, and for joy thereof goeth, and selleth all that he hath, and buyeth that field.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Again the kingdom of heaven is like to a merchant seeking good pearls.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Who when he had found one pearl of great price, went his way, and sold all that he had, and bought it.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Again the kingdom of heaven is like to a net cast into the sea, and gathering together of all kinds of fishes.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Which, when it was filled, they drew out, and sitting by the shore, they chose out the good into vessels, but the bad they cast forth.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: So shall it be at the end of the world. The angels shall go out, and shall separate the wicked from among the just.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And shall cast them into the furnace of fire: there shall be weeping and gnashing of teeth.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Have ye understood all these things? They say to him: Yes.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: He said unto them: Therefore every scribe instructed in the kingdom of heaven, is like to a man that is a householder, who bringeth forth out of his treasure new things and old.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And it came to pass: when Jesus had finished these parables, he passed from thence.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And coming into his own country, he taught them in their synagogues, so that they wondered and said: How came this man by this wisdom and miracles?

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Is not this the carpenter’s son? Is not his mother called Mary, and his brethren James, and Joseph, and Simon, and Jude:

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And his sisters, are they not all with us? Whence therefore hath he all these things?

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And they were scandalized in his regard. But Jesus said to them: A prophet is not without honour, save in his own country, and in his own house.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And he wrought not many miracles there, because of their unbelief.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: At that time Herod the Tetrarch heard the fame of Jesus.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And he said to his servants: This is John the Baptist: he is risen from the dead, and therefore mighty works shew forth themselves in him.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: For Herod had apprehended John and bound him, and put him into prison, because of Herodias, his brother’s wife.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: For John said to him: It is not lawful for thee to have her.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And having a mind to put him to death, he feared the people: because they esteemed him as a prophet.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: But on Herod’s birthday, the daughter of Herodias danced before them: and pleased Herod.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Whereupon he promised with an oath, to give her whatsoever she would ask of him.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: But she being instructed before by her mother, said: Give me here in a dish the head of John the Baptist.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And the king was struck sad: yet because of his oath, and for them that sat with him at table, he commanded it to be given.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And he sent, and beheaded John in the prison.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And his head was brought in a dish: and it was given to the damsel, and she brought it to her mother.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And his disciples came and took the body, and buried it, and came and told Jesus.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Which when Jesus had heard, he retired from thence by a boat, into a desert place apart, and the multitudes having heard of it, followed him on foot out of the cities.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And he coming forth saw a great multitude, and had compassion on them, and healed their sick.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And when it was evening, his disciples came to him, saying: This is a desert place, and the hour is now passed: send away the multitudes, that going into the towns, they may buy themselves victuals.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: But Jesus said to them, They have no need to go: give you them to eat.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: They answered him: We have not here, but five loaves, and two fishes.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Who said to them: Bring them hither to me.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And when he had commanded the multitude to sit down upon the grass, he took the five loaves and the two fishes, and looking up to heaven, he blessed, and brake, and gave the loaves to his disciples, and the disciples to the multitudes.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And they did all eat, and were filled. And they took up what remained, twelve full baskets of fragments.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And the number of them that did eat, was five thousand men, besides women and children.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And forthwith Jesus obliged his disciples to go up into the boat, and to go before him over the water, till he dismissed the people.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And having dismissed the multitude, he went into a mountain alone to pray. And when it was evening, he was there alone.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: But the boat in the midst of the sea was tossed with the waves: for the wind was contrary.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And in the fourth watch of the night, he came to them walking upon the sea.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And they seeing him walking upon the sea, were troubled, saying: It is an apparition. And they cried out for fear.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And immediately Jesus spoke to them, saying: Be of good heart: it is I, fear ye not.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And Peter making answer, said: Lord, if it be thou, bid me come to thee upon the waters.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And he said: Come. And Peter going down out of the boat walked upon the water to come to Jesus.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: But seeing the wind strong, he was afraid: and when he began to sink, he cried out, saying: Lord, save me.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And immediately Jesus stretching forth his hand took hold of him, and said to him: O thou of little faith, why didst thou doubt?

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And when they were come up into the boat, the wind ceased.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And they that were in the boat came and adored him, saying: Indeed thou art the Son of God.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And having passed the water, they came into the country of Genesar.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And when the men of that place had knowledge of him, they sent into all that country, and brought to him all that were diseased.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And they besought him that they might touch but the hem of his garment. And as many as touched, were made whole.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Then came to him from Jerusalem scribes and Pharisees, saying:

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Why do thy disciples transgress the tradition of the ancients? For they wash not their hands when they eat bread.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: But he answering, said to them: Why do you also transgress the commandment of God for your tradition? For God said:

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Honour thy father and mother: And: He that shall curse father or mother, let him die the death.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: But you say: Whosoever shall say to father or mother, The gift whatsoever proceedeth from me, shall profit thee.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And he shall not honour his father or his mother: and you have made void the commandment of God for your tradition.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Hypocrites, well hath Isaias prophesied of you, saying:

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: This people honoureth me with their lips: but their heart is far from me.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And in vain do they worship me, teaching doctrines and commandments of men.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And having called together the multitudes unto him, he said to them: Hear ye and understand.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Not that which goeth into the mouth defileth a man: but what cometh out of the mouth, this defileth a man.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Then came his disciples, and said to him: Dost thou know that the Pharisees, when they heard this word, were scandalized?

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: But he answering, said: Every plant which my heavenly Father hath not planted, shall be rooted up.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Let them alone: they are blind, and leaders of the blind. And if the blind lead the blind, both fall into the pit.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And Peter answering, said to him: Expound to us this parable.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: But he said: Are you also yet without understanding?

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Do you not understand, that whatsoever entereth into the mouth, goeth into the belly, and is cast out into the privy?

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: But the things which proceed out of the mouth, come forth from the heart, and those things defile a man.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: For from the heart come forth evil thoughts, murders, adulteries, fornications, thefts, false testimonies, blasphemies.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: These are the things that defile a man. But to eat with unwashed hands doth not defile a man.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And Jesus went from thence, and retired into the coasts of Tyre and Sidon.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And behold a woman of Canaan who came out of those coasts, crying out, said to him: Have mercy on me, O Lord, thou son of David: my daughter is grievously troubled by a devil.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Who answered her not a word. And his disciples came and besought him, saying: Send her away, for she crieth after us:

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And he answering, said: I was not sent but to the sheep, that are lost of the house of Israel.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: But she came and adored him, saying: Lord, help me.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Who answering, said: It is not good to take the bread of the children, and to cast it to the dogs.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: But she said: Yea, Lord; for the whelps also eat of the crumbs that fall from the table of their masters.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Then Jesus answering, said to her: O woman, great is thy faith: be it done to thee as thou wilt: and her daughter was cured from that hour.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And when Jesus had passed away from thence, he came nigh the sea of Galilee: and going up into a mountain, he sat there.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And there came to him great multitudes, having with them the dumb, the blind, the lame, the maimed, and many others: and they cast them down at his feet, and he healed them:

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: So that the multitudes marvelled seeing the dumb speak, the lame walk, the blind see: and they glorified the God of Israel.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And Jesus called together his disciples, and said: I have compassion on the multitudes, because they continue with me now three days, and have not what to eat, and I will not send them away fasting, lest they faint in the way.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And the disciples say unto him: Whence then should we have so many loaves in the desert, as to fill so great a multitude?

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And Jesus said to them: How many loaves have you? But they said: Seven, and a few little fishes.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And he commanded the multitude to sit down upon the ground.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And taking the seven loaves and the fishes, and giving thanks, he brake, and gave to his disciples, and the disciples gave to the people.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And they did all eat, and had their fill. And they took up seven baskets full, of what remained of the fragments.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And they that did eat, were four thousand men, beside children and women.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And having dismissed the multitude, he went up into a boat, and came into the coasts of Magedan.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And there came to him the Pharisees and Sadducees tempting: and they asked him to shew them a sign from heaven.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: But he answered and said to them: When it is evening, you say, It will be fair weather, for the sky is red.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And in the morning: To day there will be a storm, for the sky is red and lowering. You know then how to discern the face of the sky: and can you not know the signs of the times?

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: A wicked and adulterous generation seeketh after a sign: and a sign shall not be given it, but the sign of Jonas the prophet. And he left them, and went away.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And when his disciples were come over the water, they had forgotten to take bread.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Who said to them: Take heed and beware of the leaven of the Pharisees and Sadducees.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: But they thought within themselves, saying: Because we have taken no bread.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And Jesus knowing it, said: Why do you think within yourselves, O ye of little faith, for that you have no bread?

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Do you not yet understand, neither do you remember the five loaves among five thousand men, and how many baskets you took up?

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Nor the seven loaves, among four thousand men, and how many baskets you took up?

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Why do you not understand that it was not concerning bread I said to you: Beware of the leaven of the Pharisees and Sadducees?

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Then they understood that he said not that they should beware of the leaven of bread, but of the doctrine of the Pharisees and Sadducees.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And Jesus came into the quarters of Cesarea Philippi: and he asked his disciples, saying: Whom do men say that the Son of man is?

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: But they said: Some John the Baptist, and other some Elias, and others Jeremias, or one of the prophets.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Jesus saith to them: But whom do you say that I am?

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Simon Peter answered and said: Thou art Christ, the Son of the living God.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And Jesus answering said to him: Blessed art thou, Simon Bar-Jona: because flesh and blood hath not revealed it to thee, but my Father who is in heaven.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And I say to thee: That thou art Peter; and upon this rock I will build my church, and the gates of hell shall not prevail against it.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And I will give to thee the keys of the kingdom of heaven. And whatsoever thou shalt bind upon earth, it shall be bound also in heaven: and whatsoever thou shalt loose on earth, it shall be loosed also in heaven.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Then he commanded his disciples, that they should tell no one that he was Jesus the Christ.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: From that time Jesus began to shew to his disciples, that he must go to Jerusalem, and suffer many things from the ancients and scribes and chief priests, and be put to death, and the third day rise again.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And Peter taking him, began to rebuke him, saying: Lord, be it far from thee, this shall not be unto thee.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Who turning, said to Peter: Go behind me, Satan, thou art a scandal unto me: because thou savourest not the things that are of God, but the things that are of men.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Then Jesus said to his disciples: If any man will come after me, let him deny himself, and take up his cross, and follow me.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: For he that will save his life, shall lose it: and he that shall lose his life for my sake, shall find it.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: For what doth it profit a man, if he gain the whole world and suffer the loss of his own soul? Or what exchange shall a man give for his soul?

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: For the Son of man shall come in the glory of his Father with his angels: and then will he render to every man according to his works.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Amen I say to you, there are some of them that stand here, that shall not taste death, till they see the Son of man coming in his kingdom.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And after six days Jesus taketh unto him Peter and James, and John his brother, and bringeth them up into a high mountain apart:

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And he was transfigured before them. And his face did shine as the sun: and his garments became white as snow.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And behold there appeared to them Moses and Elias talking with him.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And Peter answering, said to Jesus: Lord, it is good for us to be here: if thou wilt, let us make here three tabernacles, one for thee, and one for Moses, and one for Elias.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And as he was yet speaking, behold a bright cloud overshadowed them. And lo a voice out of the cloud, saying: This is my beloved Son, in whom I am well pleased: hear ye him.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And the disciples hearing fell upon their face, and were very much afraid.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And Jesus came and touched them: and said to them: Arise, and fear not.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And they lifting up their eyes, saw no one, but only Jesus.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And as they came down from the mountain, Jesus charged them, saying: Tell the vision to no man, till the Son of man be risen from the dead.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And his disciples asked him, saying: Why then do the scribes say that Elias must come first?

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: But he answering, said to them: Elias indeed shall come, and restore all things.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: But I say to you, that Elias is already come, and they knew him not, But have done unto him whatsoever they had a mind. So also the Son of man shall suffer from them.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Then the disciples understood, that he had spoken to them of John the Baptist.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And when he was come to the multitude, there came to him a man falling down on his knees before him saying: Lord, have pity on my son, for he is a lunatic, and suffereth much: for he falleth often into the fire, and often into the water.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And I brought him to thy disciples, and they could not cure him.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Then Jesus answered and said: O unbelieving and perverse generation, how long shall I be with you? How long shall I suffer you? Bring him hither to me.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And Jesus rebuked him, and the devil went out of him, and the child was cured from that hour.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Then came the disciples to Jesus secretly, and said: Why could not we cast him out?

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Jesus said to them: Because of your unbelief. For, amen I say to you, if you have faith as a grain of mustard seed, you shall say to this mountain: Remove from hence hither, and it shall remove: and nothing shall be impossible to you.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: But this kind is not cast out but by prayer and fasting.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And when they abode together in Galilee, Jesus said to them: The Son of man shall be betrayed into the hands of men:

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And they shall kill him, and the third day he shall rise again. And they were troubled exceedingly.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And when they were come to Capharnaum, they that received the didrachmas, came to Peter, and said to him: Doth not your master pay the didrachma?

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: He said: Yes. And when he was come into the house, Jesus prevented him, saying: What is thy opinion, Simon? The kings of the earth, of whom do they receive tribute or custom, of their own children, or of strangers?

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And he said: Of strangers. Jesus said to him: Then the children are free.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: But that we may not scandalize them, go to the sea, and cast in a hook: and that fish which shall first come up, take: and when thou hast opened its mouth, thou shalt find a stater: take that, and give it to them for me and thee.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: At that hour the disciples came to Jesus, saying: Who, thinkest thou, is the greater in the kingdom of heaven?

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And Jesus, calling unto him a little child, set him in the midst of them.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And said: amen I say to you, unless you be converted, and become as little children, you shall not enter into the kingdom of heaven.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Whosoever therefore shall humble himself as this little child, he is the greater in the kingdom of heaven.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And he that shall receive one such little child in my name, receiveth me.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: But he that shall scandalize one of these little ones that believe in me, it were better for him that a millstone should be hanged about his neck, and that he should be drowned in the depth of the sea.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Woe to the world because of scandals. For it must needs be that scandals come: but nevertheless woe to that man by whom the scandal cometh.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And if thy hand, or thy foot, scandalize thee, cut it off, and cast it from thee. It is better for thee to go into life maimed or lame, than having two hands or two feet, to be cast into everlasting fire.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And if thy eye scandalize thee, pluck it out, and cast it from thee. It is better for thee having one eye to enter into life, than having two eyes to be cast into hell fire.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: See that you despise not one of these little ones: for I say to you, that their angels in heaven always see the face of my Father who is in heaven.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: For the Son of man is come to save that which was lost.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: What think you? If a man have an hundred sheep, and one of them should go astray: doth he not leave the ninety-nine in the mountains, and go to seek that which is gone astray?

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And if it so be that he find it: Amen I say to you, he rejoiceth more for that, than for the ninety-nine that went not astray.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Even so it is not the will of your Father, who is in heaven, that one of these little ones should perish.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: But if thy brother shall offend against thee, go, and rebuke him between thee and him alone. If he shall hear thee, thou shalt gain thy brother.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And if he will not hear thee, take with thee one or two more: that in the mouth of two or three witnesses every word may stand.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And if he will not hear them: tell the church. And if he will not hear the church, let him be to thee as the heathen and publican.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Amen I say to you, whatsoever you shall bind upon earth, shall be bound also in heaven: and whatsoever you shall loose upon earth, shall be loosed also in heaven.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Again I say to you, that if two of you shall consent upon earth, concerning anything whatsoever they shall ask, it shall be done to them by my Father who is in heaven.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: For where there are two or three gathered together in my name, there am I in the midst of them.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Then came Peter unto him and said: Lord, how often shall my brother offend against me, and I forgive him? till seven times?

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Jesus saith to him: I say not to thee, till seven times; but till seventy times seven times.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Therefore is the kingdom of heaven likened to a king, who would take an account of his servants.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And when he had begun to take the account, one was brought to him, that owed him ten thousand talents.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And as he had not wherewith to pay it, his lord commanded that he should be sold, and his wife and children, and all that he had, and payment to be made.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: But that servant falling down, besought him, saying: Have patience with me, and I will pay thee all.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And the lord of that servant being moved with pity, let him go and forgave him the debt.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: But when that servant was gone out, he found one of his fellow-servants that owed him an hundred pence: and laying hold of him, he throttled him, saying: Pay what thou owest.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And his fellow-servant falling down, besought him, saying: Have patience with me, and I will pay thee all.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And he would not: but went and cast him into prison, till he paid the debt.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Now his fellow servants seeing what was done, were very much grieved, and they came, and told their lord all that was done.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Then his lord called him: and said to him: Thou wicked servant, I forgave thee all the debt, because thou besoughtest me:

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Shouldst not thou then have had compassion also on thy fellow servant, even as I had compassion on thee?

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And his lord being angry, delivered him to the torturers until he paid all the debt.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: So also shall my heavenly Father do to you, if you forgive not every one his brother from your hearts.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And it came to pass when Jesus had ended these words, he departed from Galilee and came into the coasts of Judea, beyond Jordan.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And great multitudes followed him: and he healed them there.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And there came to him the Pharisees tempting him, saying: Is it lawful for a man to put away his wife for every cause?

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Who answering, said to them: Have ye not read, that he who made man from the beginning, made them male and female? And he said:

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: For this cause shall a man leave father and mother, and shall cleave to his wife, and they two shall be in one flesh.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Therefore now they are not two, but one flesh. What therefore God hath joined together, let no man put asunder.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: They say to him: Why then did Moses command to give a bill of divorce, and to put away?

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: He saith to them: Because Moses by reason of the hardness of your heart permitted you to put away your wives: but from the beginning it was not so.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And I say to you, that whosoever shall put away his wife, except it be for fornication, and shall marry another, committeth adultery: and he that shall marry her that is put away, committeth adultery.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: His disciples say unto him: If the case of a man with his wife be so, it is not expedient to marry.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Who said to them: All men take not this word, but they to whom it is given.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: For there are eunuchs, who were born so from their mother’s womb: and there are eunuchs, who were made so by men: and there are eunuchs, who have made themselves eunuchs for the kingdom of heaven. He that can take, let him take it.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Then were little children presented to him, that he should impose hands upon them and pray. And the disciples rebuked them.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: But Jesus said to them: Suffer the little children, and forbid them not to come to me: for the kingdom of heaven is for such.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And when he had imposed hands upon them, he departed from thence.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And behold one came and said to him: Good master, what good shall I do that I may have life everlasting?

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Who said to him: Why askest thou me concerning good? One is good, God. But if thou wilt enter into life, keep the commandments.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: He said to him: Which? And Jesus said: Thou shalt do no murder, Thou shalt not commit adultery, Thou shalt not steal, Thou shalt not bear false witness.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Honour thy father and thy mother: and, Thou shalt love thy neighbor as thyself.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: The young man saith to him: All these have I kept from my youth, what is yet wanting to me?

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Jesus saith to him: If thou wilt be perfect, go sell what thou hast, and give to the poor, and thou shalt have treasure in heaven: and come, follow me.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And when the young man had heard this word, he went away sad: for he had great possessions.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Then Jesus said to his disciples: Amen, I say to you, that a rich man shall hardly enter into the kingdom of heaven.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And again I say to you: It is easier for a camel to pass through the eye of a needle, than for a rich man to enter into the kingdom of heaven.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And when they had heard this, the disciples wondered much, saying: Who then can be saved?

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And Jesus beholding, said to them: With men this is impossible: but with God all things are possible.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Then Peter answering, said to him: Behold we have left all things, and have followed thee: what therefore shall we have?

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And Jesus said to them: Amen I say to you, that you who have followed me, in the regeneration, when the Son of man shall sit on the seat of his majesty, you also shall sit on twelve seats judging the twelve tribes of Israel.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And every one that hath left house, or brethren, or sisters, or father, or mother, or wife, or children, or lands for my name’s sake, shall receive an hundredfold, and shall possess life everlasting.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And many that are first, shall be last: and the last shall be first.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: The kingdom of heaven is like to an householder, who went out early in the morning to hire labourers into his vineyard.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And having agreed with the labourers for a penny a day, he sent them into his vineyard.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And going out about the third hour, he saw others standing in the marketplace idle.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And he said to them: Go you also into my vineyard, and I will give you what shall be just.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And they went their way. And again he went out about the sixth and the ninth hour, and did in like manner.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: But about the eleventh hour he went out and found others standing, and he saith to them: Why stand you here all the day idle?

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: They say to him: Because no man hath hired us. He saith to them: Go ye also into my vineyard.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And when evening was come, the lord of the vineyard saith to his steward: Call the labourers and pay them their hire, beginning from the last even to the first.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: When therefore they were come that came about the eleventh hour, they received every man a penny.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: But when the first also came, they thought that they should receive more: And they also received every man a penny.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And receiving it they murmured against the master of the house,

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Saying: These last have worked but one hour, and thou hast made them equal to us, that have borne the burden of the day and the heats.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: But he answering said to one of them: friend, I do thee no wrong: didst thou not agree with me for a penny?

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Take what is thine, and go thy way: I will also give to this last even as to thee.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Or, is it not lawful for me to do what I will? Is thy eye evil, because I am good?

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: So shall the last be first and the first last. For many are called but few chosen.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And Jesus going up to Jerusalem, took the twelve disciples apart and said to them:

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Behold we go up to Jerusalem, and the Son of man shall be betrayed to the chief priests and the scribes: and they shall condemn him to death.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And shall deliver him to the Gentiles to be mocked and scourged and crucified: and the third day he shall rise again.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Then came to him the mother of the sons of Zebedee with her sons, adoring and asking something of him.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Who said to her: What wilt thou? She saith to him: say that these my two sons may sit, the one on thy right hand, and the other on thy left, in thy kingdom.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And Jesus answering, said: You know not what you ask. Can you drink the chalice that I shall drink? They say to him: We can.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: He saith to them: My chalice indeed you shall drink; but to sit on my right or left hand is not mine to give to you, but to them for whom it is prepared by my Father.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And the ten, hearing it, were moved with indignation against the two brethren.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: But Jesus called them to him and said: You know that the princes of the Gentiles lord it over them; and that they that are the greater, exercise power upon them.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: It shall not be so among you: but whosoever is the greater among you, let him be your minister.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And he that will be first among you shall be your servant.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Even as the Son of man is not come to be ministered unto, but to minister and to give his life a redemption for many.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And when they went out from Jericho, a great multitude followed him.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And behold two blind men sitting by the way side heard that Jesus passed by. And they cried out, saying: O Lord, thou son of David, have mercy on us.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And the multitude rebuked them that they should hold their peace. But they cried out the more, saying: O Lord, thou son of David, have mercy on us.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And Jesus stood and called them and said: What will ye that I do to you?

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: They say to him: Lord, that our eyes be opened.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And Jesus having compassion on them, touched their eyes. And immediately they saw and followed him.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And when they drew nigh to Jerusalem and were come to Bethphage, unto mount Olivet, then Jesus sent two disciples,

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Saying to them: Go ye into the village that is over against you: and immediately you shall find an ass tied and a colt with her. Loose them and bring them to me.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And if any man shall say anything to you, say ye that the Lord hath need of them. And forthwith he will let them go.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Now all this was done that it might be fulfilled which was spoken by the prophet, saying:

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Tell ye the daughter of Sion: Behold thy king cometh to thee, meek and sitting upon an ass and a colt, the foal of her that is used to the yoke.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And the disciples going, did as Jesus commanded them.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And they brought the ass and the colt and laid their garments upon them and made him sit thereon.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And a very great multitude spread their garments in the way: and others cut boughs from the trees and strewed them in the way.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And the multitudes that went before and that followed cried, saying: Hosanna to the son of David: Blessed is he that cometh in the name of the Lord: Hosanna in the highest.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And when he was come into Jerusalem, the whole city was moved, saying: Who is this?

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And the people said: This is Jesus, the prophet from Nazareth of Galilee.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And Jesus went into the temple of God and cast out all them that sold and bought in the temple and overthrew the tables of the money changers and the chairs of them that sold doves.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And he saith to them: It is written, My house shall be called the house of prayer; but you have made it a den of thieves.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And there came to him the blind and the lame in the temple: and he healed them.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And the chief priests and scribes, seeing the wonderful things that he did and the children crying in the temple and saying: Hosanna to the son of David, were moved with indignation,

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And said to him: Hearest thou what these say? And Jesus said to them: Yea, have you never read: Out of the mouth of infants and of sucklings thou hast perfected praise?

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And leaving them, he went out of the city into Bethania and remained there.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And in the morning, returning into the city, he was hungry.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And seeing a certain fig tree by the way side, he came to it and found nothing on it but leaves only. And he saith to it: May no fruit grow on thee henceforward for ever. And immediately the fig tree withered away.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And the disciples seeing it wondered, saying: How is it presently withered away?

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And Jesus answering, said to them: Amen, I say to you, if you shall have faith and stagger not, not only this of the fig tree shall you do, but also if you shall say to this mountain, Take up and cast thyself into the sea, it shall be done.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And all things whatsoever you shall ask in prayer believing, you shall receive.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And when he was come into the temple, there came to him, as he was teaching, the chief priests and ancients of the people, saying: By what authority dost thou these things? And who hath given thee this authority?

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Jesus answering, said to them: I also will ask you one word, which if you shall tell me, I will also tell you by what authority I do these things.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: The baptism of John, whence was it? From heaven or from men? But they thought within themselves, saying:

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: If we shall say, from heaven, he will say to us: Why then did you not believe him? But if we shall say, from men, we are afraid of the multitude: for all held John as a prophet.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And answering Jesus, they said: We know not. He also said to them: Neither do I tell you by what authority I do these things.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: But what think you? A certain man had two sons: and coming to the first, he said: Son, go work to day in my vineyard.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And he answering, said: I will not. But afterwards, being moved with repentance, he went.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And coming to the other, he said in like manner. And he answering said: I go, Sir. And he went not.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Which of the two did the father’s will? They say to him: The first. Jesus saith to them: Amen I say to you that the publicans and the harlots shall go into the kingdom of God before you.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: For John came to you in the way of justice: and you did not believe him. But the publicans and the harlots believed him: but you, seeing it, did not even afterwards repent, that you might believe him.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Hear ye another parable. There was a man, an householder, who planted a vineyard and made a hedge round about it and dug in it a press and built a tower and let it out to husbandmen and went into a strange country.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And when the time of the fruits drew nigh, he sent his servants to the husbandmen that they might receive the fruits thereof.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And the husbandmen laying hands on his servants, beat one and killed another and stoned another.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Again he sent other servants, more than the former; and they did to them in like manner.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And last of all he sent to them his son, saying: They will reverence my son.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: But the husbandmen seeing the son, said among themselves: This is the heir: come, let us kill him, and we shall have his inheritance.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And taking him, they cast him forth out of the vineyard and killed him.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: When therefore the lord of the vineyard shall come, what will he do to those husbandmen?

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: They say to him: He will bring those evil men to an evil end and will let out his vineyard to other husbandmen that shall render him the fruit in due season.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Jesus saith to them: Have you never read in the Scriptures: The stone which the builders rejected, the same is become the head of the corner? By the Lord this has been done; and it is wonderful in our eyes.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Therefore I say to you that the kingdom of God shall be taken from you and shall be given to a nation yielding the fruits thereof.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And whosoever shall fall on this stone shall be broken: but on whomsoever it shall fall, it shall grind him to powder.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And when the chief priests and Pharisees had heard his parables, they knew that he spoke of them.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And seeking to lay hands on him, they feared the multitudes, because they held him as a prophet.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And Jesus answering, spoke again in parables to them, saying:

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: The kingdom of heaven is likened to a king who made a marriage for his son.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And he sent his servants to call them that were invited to the marriage: and they would not come.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Again he sent other servants, saying: Tell them that were invited, Behold, I have prepared my dinner: my beeves and fatlings are killed, and all things are ready. Come ye to the marriage.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: But they neglected and went their ways, one to his farm and another to his merchandise.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And the rest laid hands on his servants and, having treated them contumeliously, put them to death.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: But when the king had heard of it, he was angry: and sending his armies, he destroyed those murderers and burnt their city.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Then he saith to his servants: The marriage indeed is ready; but they that were invited were not worthy.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Go ye therefore into the highways; and as many as you shall find, call to the marriage.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And his servants going forth into the ways, gathered together all that they found, both bad and good: and the marriage was filled with guests.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And the king went in to see the guests: and he saw there a man who had not on a wedding garment.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And he saith to him: Friend, how camest thou in hither not having on a wedding garment? But he was silent.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Then the king said to the waiters: Bind his hands and feet, and cast him into the exterior darkness. There shall be weeping and gnashing of teeth.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: For many are called, but few are chosen.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Then the Pharisees going, consulted among themselves how to insnare him in his speech.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And they sent to him their disciples with the Herodians, saying: Master, we know that thou art a true speaker and teachest the way of God in truth. Neither carest thou for any man: for thou dost not regard the person of men.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Tell us therefore what dost thou think? Is it lawful to give tribute to Caesar, or not?

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: But Jesus knowing their wickedness, said: Why do you tempt me, ye hypocrites?

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Shew me the coin of the tribute. And they offered him a penny.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And Jesus saith to them: Whose image and inscription is this?

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: They say to him: Caesar’s. Then he saith to them: Render therefore to Caesar the things that are Caesar’s; and to God, the things that are God’s.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And hearing this, they wondered and, leaving him, went their ways.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: That day there came to him the Sadducees, who say there is no resurrection; and asked him,

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Saying: Master, Moses said: If a man die having no son, his brother shall marry his wife and raise up issue to his brother.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Now there were with us seven brethren: and the first having married a wife, died; and not having issue, left his wife to his brother.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: In like manner the second and the third and so on, to the seventh.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And last of all the woman died also.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: At the resurrection therefore, whose wife of the seven shall she be? For they all had her.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner
