text: And Jesus answering, said to them: You err, not knowing the Scriptures nor the power of God.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: For in the resurrection they shall neither marry nor be married, but shall be as the angels of God in heaven.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And concerning the resurrection of the dead, have you not read that which was spoken by God, saying to you:

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: I am the God of Abraham and the God of Isaac and the God of Jacob? He is not the God of the dead but of the living.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And the multitudes hearing it were in admiration at his doctrine.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: But the Pharisees, hearing that he had silenced the Sadducees, came together.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And one of them, a doctor of the law, asked him, tempting him:

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Master, which is the great commandment in the law?

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Jesus said to him: Thou shalt love the Lord thy God with thy whole heart and with thy whole soul and with thy whole mind.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: This is the greatest and the first commandment.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And the second is like to this: Thou shalt love thy neighbour as thyself.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: On these two commandments dependeth the whole law and the prophets.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And the Pharisees being gathered together, Jesus asked them,

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Saying: What think you of Christ? Whose son is he? They say to him: David’s.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: He saith to them: How then doth David in spirit call him Lord, saying:

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: The Lord said to my Lord: Sit on my right hand, until I make thy enemies thy footstool?

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: If David then call him Lord, how is he his son?

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And no man was able to answer him a word: neither durst any man from that day forth ask him any more questions.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Then Jesus spoke to the multitudes and to his disciples,

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Saying: The scribes and the Pharisees have sitten on the chair of Moses.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: All things therefore whatsoever they shall say to you, observe and do: but according to their works do ye not. For they say, and do not.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: For they bind heavy and insupportable burdens and lay them on men’s shoulders: but with a finger of their own they will not move them.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And all their works they do for to be seen of men. For they make their phylacteries broad and enlarge their fringes.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And they love the first places at feasts and the first chairs in the synagogues,

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And salutations in the market place, and to be called by men, Rabbi.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: But be not you called Rabbi. For one is your master: and all you are brethren.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And call none your father upon earth; for one is your father, who is in heaven.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Neither be ye called masters: for one is your master, Christ.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: He that is the greatest among you shall be your servant.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And whosoever shall exalt himself shall be humbled: and he that shall humble himself shall be exalted.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: But woe to you, scribes and Pharisees, hypocrites, because you shut the kingdom of heaven against men: for you yourselves do not enter in and those that are going in, you suffer not to enter.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Woe to you scribes and Pharisees, hypocrites, because you devour the houses of widows, praying long prayers. For this you shall receive the greater judgment.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Woe to you, scribes and Pharisees, hypocrites, because you go round about the sea and the land to make one proselyte. And when he is made, you make him the child of hell twofold more than yourselves.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Woe to you, blind guides, that say, Whosoever shall swear by the temple, it is nothing; but he that shall swear by the gold of the temple is a debtor.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Ye foolish and blind: for whether is greater, the gold or the temple that sanctifieth the gold?

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And whosoever shall swear by the altar, it is nothing; but whosoever shall swear by the gift that is upon it is a debtor.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Ye blind: for whether is greater, the gift or the altar that sanctifieth the gift?

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: He therefore that sweareth by the altar sweareth by it and by all things that are upon it.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And whosoever shall swear by the temple sweareth by it and by him that dwelleth in it.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And he that sweareth by heaven sweareth by the throne of God and by him that sitteth thereon.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Woe to you, scribes and Pharisees, hypocrites; because you tithe mint and anise and cummin and have left the weightier things of the law: judgment and mercy and faith. These things you ought to have done and not to leave those undone.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Blind guides, who strain out a gnat and swallow a camel.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Woe to you, scribes and Pharisees, hypocrites; because you make clean the outside of the cup and of the dish, but within you are full of rapine and uncleanness.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Thou blind Pharisee, first make clean the inside of the cup and of the dish, that the outside may become clean.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Woe to you, scribes and Pharisees, hypocrites; because you are like to whited sepulchres, which outwardly appear to men beautiful but within are full of dead men’s bones and of all filthiness.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: So you also outwardly indeed appear to men just: but inwardly you are full of hypocrisy and iniquity.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Woe to you, scribes and Pharisees, hypocrites, that build the sepulchres of the prophets and adorn the monuments of the just,

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And say: If we had been in the days of our fathers, we would not have been partakers with them in the blood of the prophets.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Wherefore you are witnesses against yourselves, that you are the sons of them that killed the prophets.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Fill ye up then the measure of your fathers.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: You serpents, generation of vipers, how will you flee from the judgment of hell?

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Therefore behold I send to you prophets and wise men and scribes: and some of them you will put to death and crucify: and some you will scourge in your synagogues and persecute from city to city.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: That upon you may come all the just blood that hath been shed upon the earth, from the blood of Abel the just, even unto the blood of Zacharias the son of Barachias, whom you killed between the temple and the altar.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Amen I say to you, all these things shall come upon this generation.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Jerusalem, Jerusalem, thou that killest the prophets and stonest them that are sent unto thee, how often would I have gathered together thy children, as the hen doth gather her chickens under her wings, and thou wouldst not?

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Behold, your house shall be left to you, desolate.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: For I say to you, you shall not see me henceforth till you say: Blessed is he that cometh in the name of the Lord.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And Jesus being come out of the temple, went away. And his disciples came to shew him the buildings of the temple.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And he answering, said to them: Do you see all these things? Amen I say to you, there shall not be left here a stone upon a stone that shall not be destroyed.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And when he was sitting on mount Olivet, the disciples came to him privately, saying: Tell us when shall these things be? And what shall be the sign of thy coming and of the consummation of the world?

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And Jesus answering, said to them: Take heed that no man seduce you.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: For many will come in my name saying, I am Christ. And they will seduce many.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And you shall hear of wars and rumours of wars. See that ye be not troubled. For these things must come to pass: but the end is not yet.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: For nation shall rise against nation, and kingdom against kingdom: And there shall be pestilences and famines and earthquakes in places.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Now all these are the beginnings of sorrows.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Then shall they deliver you up to be afflicted and shall put you to death: and you shall be hated by all nations for my name’s sake.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And then shall many be scandalized and shall betray one another and shall hate one another.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And many false prophets shall rise and shall seduce many.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And because iniquity hath abounded, the charity of many shall grow cold.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: But he that shall persevere to the end, he shall be saved.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And this gospel of the kingdom shall be preached in the whole world, for a testimony to all nations: and then shall the consummation come.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: When therefore you shall see the abomination of desolation, which was spoken of by Daniel the prophet, standing in the holy place: he that readeth let him understand.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Then they that are in Judea, let them flee to the mountains:

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And he that is on the housetop, let him not come down to take any thing out of his house:

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And he that is in the field, let him not go back to take his coat.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And woe to them that are with child and that give suck in those days.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: But pray that your flight be not in the winter or on the sabbath.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: For there shall be then great tribulation, such as hath not been from the beginning of the world until now, neither shall be.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And unless those days had been shortened, no flesh should be saved: but for the sake of the elect those days shall be shortened.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Then if any man shall say to you, Lo here is Christ, or there: do not believe him.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: For there shall arise false Christs and false prophets and shall shew great signs and wonders, insomuch as to deceive (if possible) even the elect.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Behold I have told it to you, beforehand.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: If therefore they shall say to you, Behold he is in the desert: go ye not out. Behold he is in the closets: believe it not.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: For as lightning cometh out of the east and appeareth even into the west: so shall also the coming of the Son of man be.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Wheresoever the body shall be, there shall the eagles also be gathered together.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And immediately after the tribulation of those days, the sun shall be darkened and the moon shall not give her light and the stars shall fall from heaven and the powers of heaven shall be moved.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And then shall appear the sign of the Son of man in heaven. And then shall all tribes of the earth mourn: and they shall see the Son of man coming in the clouds of heaven with much power and majesty.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And he shall send his angels with a trumpet and a great voice: and they shall gather together his elect from the four winds, from the farthest parts of the heavens to the utmost bounds of them.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And from the fig tree learn a parable: When the branch thereof is now tender and the leaves come forth, you know that summer is nigh.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: So you also, when you shall see all these things, know ye that it is nigh, even at the doors.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Amen I say to you that this generation shall not pass till all these things be done.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Heaven and earth shall pass: but my words shall not pass.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: But of that day and hour no one knoweth: no, not the angels of heaven, but the Father alone.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And as in the days of Noe, so shall also the coming of the Son of man be.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: For, as in the days before the flood they were eating and drinking, marrying and giving in marriage, even till that day in which Noe entered into the ark:

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And they knew not till the flood came and took them all away: so also shall the coming of the Son of man be.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Then two shall be in the field. One shall be taken and one shall be left.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Two women shall be grinding at the mill. One shall be taken and one shall be left.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Watch ye therefore, because you know not what hour your Lord will come.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: But this know ye, that, if the goodman of the house knew at what hour the thief would come, he would certainly watch and would not suffer his house to be broken open.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Wherefore be you also ready, because at what hour you know not the Son of man will come.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Who, thinkest thou, is a faithful and wise servant, whom his lord hath appointed over his family, to give them meat in season?

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Blessed is that servant, whom when his lord shall come he shall find so doing.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Amen I say to you: he shall place him over all his goods.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: But if that evil servant shall say in his heart: My lord is long a coming:

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And shall begin to strike his fellow servants and shall eat and drink with drunkards:

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: The lord of that servant shall come in a day that he hopeth not and at an hour that he knoweth not:

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And shall separate him and appoint his portion with the hypocrites. There shall be weeping and gnashing of teeth.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Then shall the kingdom of heaven be like to ten virgins, who taking their lamps went out to meet the bridegroom and the bride.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And five of them were foolish and five wise.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: But the five foolish, having taken their lamps, did not take oil with them.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: But the wise took oil in their vessels with the lamps.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And the bridegroom tarrying, they all slumbered and slept.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And at midnight there was a cry made: Behold the bridegroom cometh. Go ye forth to meet him.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Then all those virgins arose and trimmed their lamps.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And the foolish said to the wise: Give us of your oil, for our lamps are gone out.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: The wise answered, saying: Lest perhaps there be not enough for us and for you, go ye rather to them that sell and buy for yourselves.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Now whilst they went to buy the bridegroom came: and they that were ready went in with him to the marriage. And the door was shut.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: But at last came also the other virgins, saying: Lord, Lord, open to us.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: But he answering said: Amen I say to you, I know you not.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Watch ye therefore, because you know not the day nor the hour.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: For even as a man going into a far country called his servants and delivered to them his goods;

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And to one he gave five talents, and to another two, and to another one, to every one according to his proper ability: and immediately he took his journey.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And he that had received the five talents went his way and traded with the same and gained other five.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And in like manner he that had received the two gained other two.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: But he that had received the one, going his way, digged into the earth and hid his lord’s money.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: But after a long time the lord of those servants came and reckoned with them.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And he that had received the five talents coming, brought other five talents, saying: Lord, thou didst deliver to me five talents. Behold I have gained other five over and above.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: His lord said to him: Well done, good and faithful servant, because thou hast been faithful over a few things, I will place thee over many things. Enter thou into the joy of thy lord.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And he also that had received the two talents came and said: Lord, thou deliveredst two talents to me. Behold I have gained other two.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: His lord said to him: Well done, good and faithful servant: because thou hast been faithful over a few things, I will place thee over many things. Enter thou into the joy of thy lord.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: But he that had received the one talent, came and said: Lord, I know that thou art a hard man; thou reapest where thou hast not sown and gatherest where thou hast not strewed.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And being afraid, I went and hid thy talent in the earth. Behold here thou hast that which is thine.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And his lord answering, said to him: Wicked and slothful servant, thou knewest that I reap where I sow not and gather where I have not strewed.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Thou oughtest therefore to have committed my money to the bankers: and at my coming I should have received my own with usury.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Take ye away therefore the talent from him and give it him that hath ten talents.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: For to every one that hath shall be given, and he shall abound: but from him that hath not, that also which he seemeth to have shall be taken away.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And the unprofitable servant, cast ye out into the exterior darkness. There shall be weeping and gnashing of teeth.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And when the Son of man shall come in his majesty, and all the angels with him, then shall he sit upon the seat of his majesty.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And all nations shall be gathered together before him: and he shall separate them one from another, as the shepherd separateth the sheep from the goats:

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And he shall set the sheep on his right hand, but the goats on his left.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Then shall the king say to them that shall be on his right hand: Come, ye blessed of my Father, possess you the kingdom prepared for you from the foundation of the world.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: For I was hungry, and you gave me to eat: I was thirsty, and you gave me to drink: I was a stranger, and you took me in:

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Naked, and you covered me: sick, and you visited me: I was in prison, and you came to me.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Then shall the just answer him, saying: Lord, when did we see thee hungry and fed thee: thirsty and gave thee drink?

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Or when did we see thee a stranger and took thee in? Or naked and covered thee?

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Or when did we see thee sick or in prison and came to thee?

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And the king answering shall say to them: Amen I say to you, as long as you did it to one of these my least brethren, you did it to me.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Then he shall say to them also that shall be on his left hand: Depart from me, you cursed, into everlasting fire, which was prepared for the devil and his angels.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: For I was hungry and you gave me not to eat: I was thirsty and you gave me not to drink.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: I was a stranger and you took me not in: naked and you covered me not: sick and in prison and you did not visit me.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Then they also shall answer him, saying: Lord, when did we see thee hungry or thirsty or a stranger or naked or sick or in prison and did not minister to thee?

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Then he shall answer them, saying: Amen: I say to you, as long as you did it not to one of these least, neither did you do it to me.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And these shall go into everlasting punishment: but the just, into life everlasting.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And it came to pass, when Jesus had ended all these words, he said to his disciples:

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: You know that after two days shall be the pasch: and the Son of man shall be delivered up to be crucified.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Then were gathered together the chief priests and ancients of the people, into the court of the high priest, who was called Caiphas:

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And they consulted together that by subtilty they might apprehend Jesus and put him to death.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: But they said: Not on the festival day, lest perhaps there should be a tumult among the people.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And when Jesus was in Bethania, in the house of Simon the leper,

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: There came to him a woman having an alabaster box of precious ointment and poured it on his head as he was at table.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And the disciples seeing it had indignation, saying: To what purpose is this waste?

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: For this might have been sold for much and given to the poor.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And Jesus knowing it, said to them: Why do you trouble this woman? For she hath wrought a good work upon me.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: For the poor you have always with you: but me you have not always.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: For she in pouring this ointment on my body hath done it for my burial.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Amen I say to you, wheresoever this gospel shall be preached in the whole world, that also which she hath done shall be told for a memory of her.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Then went one of the twelve, who was called Judas Iscariot, to the chief priests.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And said to them: What will you give me, and I will deliver him unto you? But they appointed him thirty pieces of silver.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And from thenceforth he sought opportunity to betray him.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And on the first day of the Azymes, the disciples came to Jesus, saying: Where wilt thou that we prepare for thee to eat the pasch?

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: But Jesus said: Go ye into the city to a certain man and say to him: The master saith, My time is near at hand. With thee I make the pasch with my disciples.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And the disciples did as Jesus appointed to them: and they prepared the pasch.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: But when it was evening, he sat down with his twelve disciples.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And whilst they were eating, he said: Amen I say to you that one of you is about to betray me.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And they being very much troubled began every one to say: Is it I, Lord?

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: But he answering said: He that dippeth his hand with me in the dish, he shall betray me.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: The Son of man indeed goeth, as it is written of him. But woe to that man by whom the Son of man shall be betrayed. It were better for him, if that man had not been born.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And Judas that betrayed him answering, said: Is it I, Rabbi? He saith to him: Thou hast said it.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And whilst they were at supper, Jesus took bread and blessed and broke and gave to his disciples and said: Take ye and eat. This is my body.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And taking the chalice, he gave thanks and gave to them, saying: Drink ye all of this.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: For this is my blood of the new testament, which shall be shed for many unto remission of sins.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And I say to you, I will not drink from henceforth of this fruit of the vine until that day when I shall drink it with you new in the kingdom of my Father.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And a hymn being said, they went out unto mount Olivet.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Then Jesus saith to them: All you shall be scandalized in me this night. For it is written: I will strike the shepherd: and the sheep of the flock shall be dispersed.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: But after I shall be risen again, I will go before you into Galilee.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And Peter answering, said to him: Although all shall be scandalized in thee, I will never be scandalized.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Jesus said to him: Amen I say to thee that in this night before the cock crow, thou wilt deny me thrice.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Peter saith to him: Yea, though I should die with thee, I will not deny thee. And in like manner said all the disciples.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Then Jesus came with them into a country place which is called Gethsemani. And he said to his disciples: Sit you here, till I go yonder and pray.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And taking with him Peter and the two sons of Zebedee, he began to grow sorrowful and to be sad.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Then he saith to them: My soul is sorrowful even unto death. Stay you here and watch with me.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And going a little further, he fell upon his face, praying and saying: My Father, if it be possible, let this chalice pass from me. Nevertheless, not as I will but as thou wilt.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And he cometh to his disciples and findeth them asleep. And he saith to Peter: What? Could you not watch one hour with me?

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Watch ye: and pray that ye enter not into temptation. The spirit indeed is willing, but the flesh weak.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Again the second time, he went and prayed, saying: My Father, if this chalice may not pass away, but I must drink it, thy will be done.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And he cometh again and findeth them sleeping: for their eyes were heavy.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And leaving them, he went again: and he prayed the third time, saying the selfsame word.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Then he cometh to his disciples and said to them: Sleep ye now and take your rest. Behold the hour is at hand: and the Son of man shall be betrayed into the hands of sinners.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Rise: let us go. Behold he is at hand that will betray me.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: As he yet spoke, behold Judas, one of the twelve, came, and with him a great multitude with swords and clubs, sent from the chief priests and the ancients of the people.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And he that betrayed him gave them a sign, saying: Whomsoever I shall kiss, that is he. Hold him fast.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And forthwith coming to Jesus, he said: Hail, Rabbi. And he kissed him.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And Jesus said to him: Friend, whereto art thou come? Then they came up and laid hands on Jesus and held him.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And behold one of them that were with Jesus, stretching forth his hand, drew out his sword: and striking the servant of the high priest, cut off his ear.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Then Jesus saith to him: Put up again thy sword into its place: for all that take the sword shall perish with the sword.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Thinkest thou that I cannot ask my Father, and he will give me presently more than twelve legions of angels?

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: How then shall the scriptures be fulfilled, that so it must be done?

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: In that same hour, Jesus said to the multitudes: You are come out, as it were to a robber, with swords and clubs to apprehend me. I sat daily with you, teaching in the temple: and you laid not hands on me.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Now all this was done that the scriptures of the prophets might be fulfilled. Then the disciples, all leaving him, fled.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: But they holding Jesus led him to Caiphas the high priest, where the scribes and the ancients were assembled.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And Peter followed him afar off, even to the court of the high priest, And going in, he sat with the servants, that he might see the end.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And the chief priests and the whole council sought false witness against Jesus, that they might put him to death.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And they found not, whereas many false witnesses had come in. And last of all there came two false witnesses:

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And they said: This man said, I am able to destroy the temple of God and after three days to rebuild it.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And the high priest rising up, said to him: Answerest thou nothing to the things which these witness against thee?

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: But Jesus held his peace. And the high priest said to him: I adjure thee by the living God, that thou tell us if thou be the Christ the Son of God.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Jesus saith to him: Thou hast said it. Nevertheless I say to you, hereafter you shall see the Son of man sitting on the right hand of the power of God and coming in the clouds of heaven.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Then the high priest rent his garments, saying: He hath blasphemed: What further need have we of witnesses? Behold, now you have heard the blasphemy.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: What think you? But they answering, said: He is guilty of death.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Then did they spit in his face and buffeted him. And others struck his face with the palms of their hands,

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Saying: Prophesy unto us, O Christ. Who is he that struck thee?

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: But Peter sat without in the court. And there came to him a servant maid, saying: Thou also wast with Jesus the Galilean.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: But he denied before them all, saying: I know not what thou sayest.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And as he went out of the gate, another maid saw him; and she saith to them that were there: This man also was with Jesus of Nazareth.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And again he denied with an oath: I know not the man.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And after a little while, they came that stood by and said to Peter: Surely thou also art one of them. For even thy speech doth discover thee.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Then he began to curse and to swear that he knew not the man. And immediately the cock crew.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And Peter remembered the word of Jesus which he had said: Before the cock crow, thou wilt deny me thrice. And going forth, he wept bitterly.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And when morning was come, all the chief priests and ancients of the people took counsel against Jesus, that they might put him to death.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And they brought him bound and delivered him to Pontius Pilate the governor.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Then Judas, who betrayed him, seeing that he was condemned, repenting himself, brought back the thirty pieces of silver to the chief priests and ancients,

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Saying: I have sinned in betraying innocent blood. But they said: What is that to us? Look thou to it.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And casting down the pieces of silver in the temple, he departed and went and hanged himself with an halter.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: But the chief priests having taken the pieces of silver, said: It is not lawful to put them into the corbona, because it is the price of blood.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And after they had consulted together, they bought with them the potter’s field, to be a burying place for strangers.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: For this cause that field was called Haceldama, that is, the field of blood, even to this day.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Then was fulfilled that which was spoken by Jeremias the prophet, saying: And they took the thirty pieces of silver, the price of him that was prized, whom they prized of the children of Israel.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And they gave them unto the potter’s field, as the Lord appointed to me.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And Jesus stood before the governor, and the governor asked him, saying: Art thou the king of the Jews? Jesus saith to him: Thou sayest it.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And when he was accused by the chief priests and ancients, he answered nothing.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Then Pilate saith to him: Dost not thou hear how great testimonies they allege against thee?

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And he answered him to never a word, so that the governor wondered exceedingly.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Now upon the solemn day the governor was accustomed to release to the people one prisoner, whom they would.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And he had then a notorious prisoner that was called Barabbas.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: They therefore being gathered together, Pilate said: Whom will you that I release to You: Barabbas, or Jesus that is called Christ?

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: For he knew that for envy they had delivered him.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And as he was sitting in the place of judgment, his wife sent to him, saying: Have thou nothing to do with that just man; for I have suffered many things this day in a dream because of him.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: But the chief priests and ancients persuaded the people that they should ask Barabbas and make Jesus away.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And the governor answering, said to them: Whether will you of the two to be released unto you? But they said: Barabbas.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Pilate saith to them: What shall I do then with Jesus that is called Christ? They say all: Let him be crucified.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: The governor said to them: Why, what evil hath he done? But they cried out the more, saying: Let him be crucified.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And Pilate seeing that he prevailed nothing, but that rather a tumult was made, taking water washed his hands before the people, saying: I am innocent of the blood of this just man. Look you to it.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And the whole people answering, said: His blood be upon us and upon our children.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Then he released to them Barabbas: and having scourged Jesus, delivered him unto them to be crucified.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Then the soldiers of the governor, taking Jesus into the hall, gathered together unto him the whole band.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And stripping him, they put a scarlet cloak about him.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And platting a crown of thorns, they put it upon his head, and a reed in his right hand. And bowing the knee before him, they mocked him, saying: Hail, King of the Jews.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And spitting upon him, they took the reed and struck his head.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And after they had mocked him, they took off the cloak from him and put on him his own garments and led him away to crucify him.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And going out, they found a man of Cyrene, named Simon: him they forced to take up his cross.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And they came to the place that is called Golgotha, which is the place of Calvary.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And they gave him wine to drink mingled with gall. And when he had tasted, he would not drink.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And after they had crucified him, they divided his garments, casting lots; that it might be fulfilled which was spoken by the prophet, saying: They divided my garments among them; and upon my vesture they cast lots.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And they sat and watched him.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And they put over his head his cause written: THIS IS JESUS THE KING OF THE JEWS.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Then were crucified with him two thieves: one on the right hand and one on the left.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And they that passed by blasphemed him, wagging their heads,

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And saying: Vah, thou that destroyest the temple of God and in three days dost rebuild it: save thy own self. If thou be the Son of God, come down from the cross.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: In like manner also the chief priests, with the scribes and ancients, mocking said:

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: He saved others: himself he cannot save. If he be the king of Israel, let him now come down from the cross: and we will believe him.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: He trusted in God: let him now deliver him if he will have him. For he said: I am the Son of God.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And the selfsame thing the thieves also that were crucified with him reproached him with.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Now from the sixth hour, there was darkness over the whole earth, until the ninth hour.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And about the ninth hour, Jesus cried with a loud voice, saying: Eli, Eli, lamma sabacthani? That is, My God, My God, why hast thou forsaken me?

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And some that stood there and heard said: This man calleth Elias.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And immediately one of them running took a sponge and filled it with vinegar and put it on a reed and gave him to drink.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And the others said: Let be. Let us see whether Elias will come to deliver him.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And Jesus again crying with a loud voice, yielded up the ghost.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And behold the veil of the temple was rent in two from the top even to the bottom: and the earth quaked and the rocks were rent.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And the graves were opened: and many bodies of the saints that had slept arose,

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And coming out of the tombs after his resurrection, came into the holy city and appeared to many.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Now the centurion and they that were with him watching Jesus, having seen the earthquake and the things that were done, were sore afraid, saying: Indeed this was the Son of God.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And there were there many women afar off, who had followed Jesus from Galilee, ministering unto him:

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Among whom was Mary Magdalen and Mary the mother of James and Joseph and the mother of the sons of Zebedee.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And when it was evening, there came a certain rich man of Arimathea, named Joseph, who also himself was a disciple of Jesus.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: He went to Pilate and asked the body of Jesus. Then Pilate commanded that the body should be delivered.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And Joseph taking the body wrapped it up in a clean linen cloth:

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And laid it in his own new monument, which he had hewed out in a rock. And he rolled a great stone to the door of the monument and went his way.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And there was there Mary Magdalen and the other Mary, sitting over against the sepulchre.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And the next day, which followed the day of preparation, the chief priests and the Pharisees came together to Pilate,

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Saying: Sir, we have remembered, that that seducer said, while he was yet alive: After three days I will rise again.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Command therefore the sepulchre to be guarded until the third day: lest perhaps his disciples come and steal him away and say to the people: He is risen from the dead. And the last error shall be worse than the first.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Pilate saith to them: You have a guard. Go, guard it as you know.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And they departing, made the sepulchre sure, sealing the stone and setting guards.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And in the end of the sabbath, when it began to dawn towards the first day of the week, came Mary Magdalen and the other Mary, to see the sepulchre.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And behold there was a great earthquake. For an angel of the Lord descended from heaven and coming rolled back the stone and sat upon it.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And his countenance was as lightning and his raiment as snow.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And for fear of him, the guards were struck with terror and became as dead men.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And the angel answering, said to the women: Fear not you: for I know that you seek Jesus who was crucified.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: He is not here. For he is risen, as he said. Come, and see the place where the Lord was laid.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And going quickly, tell ye his disciples that he is risen. And behold he will go before you into Galilee. There you shall see him. Lo, I have foretold it to you.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And they went out quickly from the sepulchre with fear and great joy, running to tell his disciples.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And behold, Jesus met them, saying: All hail. But they came up and took hold of his feet and adored him.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Then Jesus said to them: Fear not. Go, tell my brethren that they go into Galilee. There they shall see me.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Who when they were departed, behold, some of the guards came into the city and told the chief priests all things that had been done.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And they being assembled together with the ancients, taking counsel, gave a great sum of money to the soldiers,

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Saying: Say you, His disciples came by night and stole him away when we were asleep.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And if the governor shall hear of this, we will persuade him and secure you.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: So they taking the money, did as they were taught: and this word was spread abroad among the Jews even unto this day.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And the eleven disciples went into Galilee, unto the mountain where Jesus had appointed them.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And seeing him they adored: but some doubted.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And Jesus coming, spoke to them, saying: All power is given to me in heaven and in earth.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Going therefore, teach ye all nations: baptizing them in the name of the Father and of the Son and of the Holy Ghost.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Teaching them to observe all things whatsoever I have commanded you. And behold I am with you all days, even to the consummation of the world.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner
